titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/144

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=419 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:11:34.801786+00 | translation moa=420 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:11:33.107085+00

p.span:
Omnibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Walterus filius Alani Domini Regis Scocie senescallus salutem in domino . Nouerit
vniuersitas uestra me dedisse et concessisse deo et beate Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus totam terram de
Molle per rectas diuisas suas et quicquid continetur imfra easdem diuisas cum omnibus pertinenciis suis . quam tenui in feodo de domino
Willelmo de vesci et omnia iura et actiones que mihi competebant uel conpetere poterant racione eorundem siue in eisdem . in liberam .
puram . et perpetuam elemosinam ad habendam . possidendam et tenendam in perpetuum . adeo libere . quiete . plenarie et honorifice  sicut aliqua
elemosina in regno scocie liberius . quiecius plenius et honorificencius tenetur et possidetur . Nec ego nec heredes mei in eadem terra
siue in rebus contentis imfra diuisas eiusdem terre decetero in perpetuum aliquid iuris reclamabimus . Immo liberam ab omni exaccione . seruicio .
consuetudine et qualibet alia seruitute contra omnes in perpetuum eisdem monachis cum omnibus premissis warantizabimus defendemus et ma
nutenebimus . In cuius rei testimonium presens scriptum sigilli mei apposicione roboraui . Hiis testibus . Thoma Croc . Symone
filio bertulfi . Ada le Waleys . Willelmo de hauekerestun . et Roberto de Kiphau militibus . Willelmo brun Magistro Monialium de suber
wic . Willelmo et Roberto capellanis nostris . Leonio de Normannvil’ . Ricardo de muschelburc . et Iohanne de leycest’ . Monachis de Melros . et multis
aliis .

div.p: To all who shall see or hear this written deed, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord King of Scotland, sends greeting in the Lord. Let your whole community know me to have given and granted to God and to blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the whole land of Mow by its correct boundaries and whatever is contained within the same boundaries with all its pertinents which I held in feu of the lord William de Vescy and all rights and public functions which applied to me or could apply by reason of the same or in the same, in free, pure, and perpetual alms to have, possess and hold for ever, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed. Neither I nor my heirs shall in the future claim back any right in the same land or in the things contained within the boundaires of the same land for ever; but we shall warrant, defend, and maintain against all people for ever to the same monks with everything mentioned before, free from every exaction, service, custom and whatever other service. As witness of this matter I have secured the present written deed by the application of my seal. With these witnesses: Thomas Crook, Simon son of Bertolf, Adam Wallace, William of Halkerston, Robert of Kippo, knights; William Broun, master of the nuns of South Berwick; William and Robert our chaplains; Leon de Normanville, Richard of Musselburgh, and John of Leicester, monks of Melrose; and many others.
